articles.headline: Michael Carrick nailed on for permanent manager role at Man United - Jamie Carragher
articles.description: Jamie Carragher has no doubt Michael Carrick will secure the Manchester United manager's position full-time after a series of "sensational results."

articles.headline: Erling Haaland warns rivals: Man City will be even better next season
articles.description: Erling Haaland has warned the rest of the Premier League that Manchester City will be even better next season.

articles.headline: Premier League players out of contract: Who could leave your team on a free this summer?
articles.description: ESPN takes a look at the players who are out of contract in 2026 across all 20 Premier League clubs.

articles.headline: Arsenal and Man City's remaining Premier League fixtures compared as title race hots up
articles.description: The Premier League title race is well and truly underway with Arsenal being chased down by Pep Guardiola's Manchester City in their hunt for a first top-flight trophy since 2004.

articles.headline: Five times Arsenal have let the Premier League title slip away
articles.description: Arsenal's quest for their first Premier League title since the Invincibles' famous triumph in 2003-04 has recently taken a major dent.

articles.headline: Can Saints beat Ipswich to blow automatic promotion race wide open?
articles.description: It is going to be a cracker at St Mary's on Tuesday night. A season-defining game in the Championship promotion race.

articles.headline: Manchester United edge closer to precious UCL spot
articles.description: Michael Carrick's Red Devils increased their chances of competing in next season's Champions League with a hard-fought triumph against Brentford.

articles.headline: Michael Carrick has done all he can to earn Man United job
articles.description: With nine wins in 13 games and a Champions League place all but secure, Michael Carrick has made a convincing case to be Man United's coach next season.

articles.headline: Man United shouldn't 'over celebrate' Champions League place - Michael Carrick
articles.description: Michael Carrick has said Manchester United should not "over celebrate" reaching the Champions League after the 2-1 win over Brentford put the club on the brink of a return to Europe's top competition.

articles.headline: Melchiot: Chelsea need a coach who stands up for himself
articles.description: Mario Melchiot says Chelsea's next coach must "stand up for himself" and not simply follow what he's told.

articles.headline: Phil Jones: Maguire has been exceptional
articles.description: Former Manchester United defender Phil Jones praises Manchester United's defence, but says performances "haven't been vintage".

articles.headline: Nicol: Arteta can't say 'no excuses' then complain about red cards
articles.description: Steve Nicol reacts after Mikel Arteta criticized two decisions not given as red cards.

articles.headline: Phil Jones: Carrick should get Man United job full-time
articles.description: Speaking on ESPN FC, former Manchester United defender Phil Jones calls for interim manager Michael Carrick to be given the job permanently.

articles.headline: Sesko scores as Manchester Utd bolster UCL spot in win
articles.description: Manchester United moved within touching distance of Champions League qualification as captain Bruno Fernandes edged closer to the Premier League assist record in Monday's 2-1 victory against Brentford.

articles.headline: Casemiro and Sesko on target to boost Man Utd chances of CL return
articles.description: Highlights from the Premier League match between Manchester United and Brentford.

articles.headline: Man Utd edge closer to Champions League return after Brentford win
articles.description: Manchester United are within touching distance of returning to the Champions League after beating Brentford 2-1 at Old Trafford.

articles.headline: Papers: Three-man shortlist emerges for Chelsea job
articles.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Tuesday's newspapers...

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal keeping tabs on Madrid's Endrick
articles.description: Arsenal are among several clubs monitoring Real Madrid striker Endrick. Transfer Talk has the latest.

articles.headline: PSG's Luis Enrique only coach to tick every box for Man United
articles.description: Manchester United have settled on two candidates for the permanent manager's job at Old Trafford: Michael Carrick or a unicorn.

articles.headline: Ogden: Why Luis Enrique is Man United's 'unicorn' option
articles.description: Mark Ogden explains why a manager like PSG's Luis Enrique would be an ideal successor to Michael Carrick at Manchester United

articles.headline: Ogden: Chelsea's players deserved a better coach than Rosenior
articles.description: Mark Ogden explains why he has sympathy for any player wishing to leave Chelsea after the sacking of Liam Rosenior.

articles.headline: Chelsea reach FA Cup final, another ref scandal for Serie A, more
articles.description: Chelsea advanced to the FA Cup final days after changing managers (again), but it's hardly proof of the club's "project" working. Plus: Is Serie A heading for another referee scandal, and can Arsenal hang on in the English title race?

articles.headline: Gab & Juls can't believe VAR missed a penalty for Everton vs. West Ham
articles.description: Gab Marcotti and Julien Laurens criticise Premier League officiating after appearing to miss a handball call against West Ham's Mateus Fernandes against Everton.

articles.headline: Tottenham's Xavi Simons ruled out for rest of season, to miss World Cup
articles.description: Tottenham forward Xavi Simons will play no further part this season and miss the World Cup this summer after a suspected anterior cruciate ligament injury in Spurs' win over Wolves.

articles.headline: Laurens questions why Spurs' Simons attempted to run off an ACL injury
articles.description: Julien Laurens feels for Tottenham's Xavi Simons, who will miss the end of the season and the World Cup with an ACL injury.

articles.headline: Gary Neville: Arsenal have better chance of winning Premier League than Champions League
articles.description: Gary Neville has said Arsenal have a better chance of winning the Premier League than the Champions League this season.

articles.headline: McFarlane: Reaching FA Cup final completely changes feel at Chelsea
articles.description: Calum McFarlane speaks about the importance of Chelsea's FA Cup semifinal win over Leeds.

articles.headline: Man United vs. Brentford: TV channel, kick-off time, live stream, referee, injury and team news
articles.description: Everything you need to know about Manchester United vs. Brentford, including five key talking points.

articles.headline: Are Tottenham going to be relegated from the Premier League? What stats, charts say
articles.description: For Tottenham fans, the team's nosedive into the relegation zone has forced them to think the unthinkable - are they really going to go down?

articles.headline: Pep gives Manchester City license for quality time on break
articles.description: Pep Guardiola has given his treble-chasing players permission to jet off around the world before Manchester City's final push.

articles.headline: Michael Carrick backs Manchester United's defensive options despite availability crisis
articles.description: Michael Carrick says he does not have concerns about Manchester United's central defensive options heading into what looks to be a big summer at Old Trafford.

articles.headline: Rosenior's villain turns Chelsea hero: Enzo Fernández books FA Cup final spot
articles.description: No player embodied Liam Rosenior's hapless three-month reign like Enzo Fernández. On Sunday, the midfielder was excellent in guiding Chelsea to the FA Cup final.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United monitor AC Milan's Rafael Leão
articles.description: AC Milan winger Rafael Leão is subject to attention from Manchester United, while Tijjani Reijnders could leave Manchester City this summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

articles.headline: Benjamin Šeško says he wants to win trophies with Man United
articles.description: Manchester United forward Benjamin Šeško has said he hopes a strong finish to the season can fuel a future push for trophies.
